title: Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis
intro: Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis, also known as the hybrid Rhaphiolepis, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its showy pink or white flowers and fragrant leaves. The leaves and flowers of Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis have been traditionally used in medicine, providing health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis is used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis is classified as a hybrid plant, specifically a cross between two species of the Rhaphiolepis genus. It belongs to the family Rosaceae, which includes many other flowering plants commonly known as roses and their relatives. The Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis is further categorized as a shrub, with characteristics such as having evergreen leaves and producing showy flowers. As a member of the Rosaceae family, it shares common traits with other plants in the same family. Through its classification, the Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis is placed within the broader plant kingdom.

The plant is known to contain various flavonoids and phenolic acids that may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, potentially contributing to its health benefits.
